reviews.review_text:
Is this game fun? Yes, it can definitely be fun. Is it a good Resident Evil game? Well, there are definitely some survivor horror elements to it and a lot of the staples from recent Resident Evil games make appearances so I would say yes (especially on higher difficulties). Is it a good representation of Resident Evil 3? Far from it.

Honestly, this will always be one of the biggest fumbles that Capcom has ever done in my book. I remember getting this game way back when it first came out on Xbox. My friends and I were very excited for this game as RE2's remake was phenomenal and thought it was going to be of the same quality. We were all pretty damn disappointed, especially as most of us in the party chat at the time had beaten or at least played the original release. Most of us were able to beat the game in a matter of hours, under 3 hours in one sitting in most cases. We were shocked at the short length of the game and felt ripped off. Upon revisiting the game recently I came away with those same feelings and 100%ing this game was a sad and disappointing chore.

Now, there are plenty of articles and videos and other reviews which go into excruciating detail about how horribly this release represents the story of RE3. I will not beat a dead horse here and waste anyone's time with a sermon, if you want to see how much we were robbed in detail the truth is out there. Glaring examples include the scripting of Nemesis, character assassination of Jill, omission of dynamic gameplay choices (go in the RPD station or stay and fight the monster), and personally the lack of the original Mercenaries mode. In short, the game is a hollow shell of the game it is meant to be a remake of. This game's omission of content and short run time genuinely have me feeling like this should have been DLC for RE2 remake. Crazy take, I know, but that is the feeling I got from this game. If they weren't going to put the effort in, why make the full game in the first place?

Now, apart from being a fan of the original game and hating how the story is represented here, I have to be honest with the gameplay itself. For all the narrative and thematic shortcomings this game has, I found zero performance issues with the game. From running it back at launch to running it on my desktop and Steam Deck (it works very well in handheld) the game ran absolutely fine. Zero bugs or frustrations to be had. Graphics were solid and the game looked great. This is to be expected as the work that went into RE2's remake set the stage for this release to be solid performance-wise. Just gotta be honest with this part at the very least.

Okay, time to sum this all up. If you like the original RE3 and care about stories and how characters are treated and portrayed you will come away from this one disappointed. If you simply want a Resident Evil flavored game which is essentially less than half of the game it is a remake of and simply want solid gameplay and a short story, you can find enjoyment here. This is truly one of the games of all time, I would probably have enjoyed it a lot better if it wasn't claiming to be a remake of RE3. This game is a solid (and charitable) 5/10 and is truly a prominent example of wasted potential, especially given how amazing RE2 and RE4's remakes were.
It was good. Definitely felt a bit like an RE2 DLC, but it had its charm, and who doesn't love Jill?

Definitely overhated game. Gameplay is good . Main drawbacks of it are the Playtime and Nemesis. Nemesis felt like a scripted scene not a stalker. If you plan on buying it. Buy it at a discount. Never at full price

As someone who has never played the original Resident Evil 3: Nemesis, I had a good time with Resident Evil 3 Remake. It seems that most of the haters for this game are folks who've played and prefer the original.

Outside of some campy dialogue, trope-y girlboss moments, ridiculous cutscenes & quick-time events, and how short/easy the game was, I thought this was a good continuation from Resident Evil 2 Remake; though it does feels more like a DLC expansion to Resident Evil 2 Remake than what you'd expect from mainline Resident Evil entry.

I almost skipped it to go straight to Resident Evil 4 Remake, but was able to knock it out over a weekend because it was so short. I'd say it's worth picking up on sale.

I finished RE3 yesterday. and I was really surprised, cuz the game itself is too short. In less than six hours, with me dying 3 times, and exploring the whole map, I still finished it in under six hours. However, this is a really good game. You've Nemesis chasing you from the beginning to the end, and when it appears the game becomes a horror game, Cuz Nemesis is fast and has strong punch combos. Nemesis has some weird variants, but the first one really reminds me the old school Nemesis. 

The puzzles are really easy, It took less than 5 minutes to solve them, like the train station puzzle. I really missed harder puzzles. 

One thing that bothered me is that the maps are very small, it's almost impossible to get lost in this RE. It's so linear and obvious where the objectives and items are, and sometimes the items appears on the map. I think they made it better on RE2 Remake. The enemy variety also could've been better cuz we don't really have that many enemy types. 

We also have a good amount of ammo. Specially if you're not blind with a gun.

The biggest problem with this RE is that is too fast. Small maps, short story, and too few enemies. And you also can dodge attacks, which makes the game very very easy. 

I don't think it's worth the full price. I you really want the game, I suggest waiting for a sale. The game could've been better, but is a good game.

It is impossible to recommend this game for many reasons. The game as a game isn't that bad, but when I consider how short the whole experience is and how much they cut from the original, I just don't want to support this arrogance in any way.

The scenes with Nemesis have that Hollywood sensationalism, but they are completely devoid of meaning, because in 95% of cases they are completely scripted. 

I do not want to recommend this game of principle to anyone. Buy it at a huge discount (I grabbed it for 3e) only if you're a big fan of the Resident Evil series. If you are not fan, play literally any other modern Resident Evil game and you'll get everything you get here, only significantly better and longer.

If you really want to experience what Resident Evil 3 has to offer in terms of lore, then just play the original, it's far superior as it is even with all the obsolescence that is present.
Fun game, but that Nemesis was a pain in the ass and the dodge roll was meh.

People might say that it's a very short game, they might also say that it cut a lot of stuff from the original and they are absolutely right, but I feel like the game is still fun and I actually loved the combat a lot more than in Resident Evil 2RE. The cutscenes are also a lot better imo but the storytelling was probably a bit better in RE2RE.

A good game to play, short though.
If you like Resident Evil Games its worth a try.

[h3] Graphics: 4/5
Gameplay: 3/5
Story: 3/5[/h3]

[i]Resident Evil 3 shines with its open map design, offering more variety and new locations compared to Resident Evil 2, which feels confined to just a couple of indoor settings. The RE Engine once again proves its worth with stunning graphics and smooth gameplay that make exploring Raccoon City a thrill. 

However, the game falls short in a few key areas. Nemesis, instead of being a terrifying stalker, is reduced to repetitive boss fights that quickly become frustrating. The overall campaign is short, feeling more like a narrative bridge between entries in the series rather than a fully fleshed-out survival horror experience.[/i]

Although the new dodging mechanic is buggy as ♥♥♥♥, the game is still INCREDIBLY GOOD. There’s significantly less content compared to RE2 (at least that’s how it feels after finishing it), but it’s more dynamic and interesting. In my opinion, it’s not better than RE2, but it’s not much worse either (the plot in RE3 is sometimes a bit cringeworthy; Jill would have died 6–7 times (from injuries, rocket shrapnel, etc.) if the game’s events had happened in real life). 8+/10
This is my third game in the RE series - next up is RE4 (the remake).
it could have been longer but for the first resi title ive ever played since being a huge silent hill girlie it was a very enjoyable game. and jill is soooo ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hot. that woman... yummy.

As a big fan of the Resident Evil Franchise, I am afraid to say that I am a bit dissapointed. Not because of how the game ran, the story or anything that you could think of. It's the fact that it is listed as a 40euro game. 40euros is a lot, especially in this economy. 40euros is equivalent to around 2 days worth of work, but I would like to say I liked the game. Even thought it was only about 4 hours and 16mins of my life. For 40 euros. That is why, I would like to advise you on buying this game **only** when it goes on !_sale_!. 
Thank you and I am really hopping that the 4th Resident Evil will not dissapoint me in such way.

Sincerely,
gamergirlXOXO
this game is soo over hated its mad its a very good amazing game the only downside is its so short my first playthrough was only 3 hoursbut its still a good game and worth playing

Good game to get in promo, I had lots of fun specially playing it after RE2R. The biggest flaw of RE3R is its short length and lack of content. It's not a game worth paying full price for, but it's worth it on sale. Some might say that what's being sold is REverse, RE3R is a "freebie".

Meu RE preferido desde o original, apesar de ser bem curto vale a pena zerar várias vezes. Bem fácil de platinar...
 
Nemesis em 4K FullHD... Jogaço!

Honestly amazing game and short too, to anyone that likes the Resident Evil series I truly recommend this game. I loved this game so much especially the Nemesis chases and fights. I hope this review reaches out to the people who need this.

Good gameplay, ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ story

Nikolai is genuinely one of the most ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ villains I've ever experienced in a video game. Genuinely the worst motivation for a villain I've ever heard in my life.

Gameplay is fun, Horror is Good, I like Nemesis
♥♥♥♥ the story of this game though ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, I was so disappointed with how the plot progresses after halfway through the game.

If you're playing this to go through the story of all the Resident Evil games, just read a synopsis online.

[h1]RE:2보다 못한 전략성과 공포, RE:4보다 못한 액션 [/h1]

//스포일러 주의

[h3]탄약 관리와 약점 공략의 재미가 핵심이라는 것을 잊은 RE:3 [/h3]

RE:2의 공포와 압박감은 한정된 탄약에서 오고, 재미는 그 한정된 탄약을 어떻게 쓸지에서 왔다. 좀비로 가로막힌 구간을 돌파해야 할 때, 나는 세 가지 선택을 할 수 있다. 헤드샷 한 발로 좀비를 그로기로 만들고 빠르게 지나가거나, 다리에 세 발을 쏴서 부숴버리고 넘어진 좀비를 지나가거나, 또는 탄약을 쏟아부어 아예 죽여버리는 방법이 있다. 헤드샷의 경우엔 가장 탄약을 아낄 수 있지만, 빠르게 해당 구간을 지나가지 않으면 포위당할 위험이 있다. 다리를 부수면 좀비의 공격성을 현저히 낮출 수 있어 제법 안전해지지만, 좀비는 여전히 살아있기에 다리를 물리지 않게 계속 긴장하며 다녀야 한다. 좀비 한 마리를 완전히 죽이는 경우는 이후에 소모한 탄약 만큼의 좀비를 무력화시킬 수 없다는 것을 의미했다. 따라서 나는 항상 빠르게 상황을 판단하여 한정된 자원을 어떻게 써야 할지 계산하는 자원 관리의 즐거움과 더불어 자원의 양에서 오는 압박감으로 인한 긴장감을 유지할 수 있었다. 

RE:4는 헤드샷-근접공격, 패리 등을 이용한 액션의 쾌감을 극대화하는 데 집중했다. 헤드샷을 맞추면 확정적으로 큰 대미지를 주는 근접공격이 가능한 것을 이용해 여러 적을 동시에 타격할 수 있게 유도하거나, 거리를 잘못 조절해도 빠르게 반응하여 패리를 하는 것으로 위기를 기회로 만들거나, 적의 손에 든 폭탄이나 화염병을 맞춰 주변의 적을 한꺼번에 쓸어버리는 쾌감을 선사했다. 

그러나 RE:3는 RE:2의 공포와 전략성도, RE:4의 손맛도 챙기지 못했다. 탄약은 항상 넉넉하게 주어져 관리하는 의미가 없었고, 헤드샷으로 죽이는 데도 네 다섯 발, 다리를 부수는 데도 네 다섯 발이 소모되니 머리 이외의 부위를 노릴 이유도 없었다. 그저 머리가 약점인 좀비가 나오는 평범한 좀비 게임으로 전락하고 만 것이다. 새로 추가된 회피 시스템 역시 그저 RE:2에는 없었으니 추가한 계륵과도 같은 기믹으로 느껴졌다. 일반 좀비 상대로는 쓸 이유가 없고, 특수 좀비의 경우도 개구리 헌터는 회피로 피해서 지나가려 하면 무조건 즉사기에 당하고, 도마뱀 헌터는 나름 재미있는 기믹일 수도 있었지만 적중 시 도마뱀 헌터가 뒤로 넘어지는 바람에 오히려 약점인 머리를 쏠 수가 없는데다 즉사기의 존재도 있는 등 적의 특징이 게임의 시스템과 부드럽게 맞물린다는 느낌이 들지는 않았다. 먹먹한 사운드도 아쉬운 타격감에 한 몫 했다. 


[h3]내러티브도, 보스전의 재미도 못 챙긴 네메시스[/h3]

네메시스는 질의 아파트를 갑자기 파괴하며 등장하고, 육체만으로 벽을 뚫어가며 질을 추격하는 위압감 넘치는 모습을 보여줬다. 그러나 이후에는 엎어지면 코 닿을 거리에서 중화기를 들지 않나, 몸에 불이 붙어 강물에 뛰어들지 않나, 주인공들의 대응에 족족 당하질 않나, 계속해서 추하고 멍청한 모습을 보여주어 마치 짜증나는 유치원생이 달라붙는 것 마냥 귀찮게만 느껴졌다. 차라리 압도적인 힘을 가지고 있지만 그게 건물을 부술 정도는 안 되어, 질 일행이 건물로 대피해 안심해 있을 때, 로켓런처로 건물을 날려버리며 계속 추격했다면, 중화기까지 쓰는 끔찍한 추격자의 위용을 뽐낼 수 있지 않았을까. 

보스전 때도 추격 시의 날렵한 모습은 온데간데 없고 느리고 힘없는 공격만 할 뿐이었다. 게다가 추격 시에 총이 잘 안 통하는 걸 확인시켰음에도 보스전 때는 총을 써야 하니 총으로 공격하는 게 맞는 건지 계속해서 의심이 들게 했다. 첫 번째 보스전은 RE:2의 첫 번째 보스전과 비슷하면서도, RE:2의 보스가 좁은 공간에서 주변 사물을 스파크가 튈 정도로 마구 헤집으며 달려드는 위압감을 보여준 반면 네메시스는 굼뜬 동작과 넓은 필드의 시너지로 긴장감이 전혀 없었다. 두 번째 보스전부터는 높은 곳에서 추락이라는 숨겨진 약점이 생기는데, 나는 그것을 꽤 오랜 시간이 걸려서야 우연히 발견했다. 그도 그럴게 그 덩치에 육체만으로 건물을 부수는 힘을 가진 괴물이 고작 2층 높이의 건물에서 떨어졌다고 그로기에 걸릴 것이라고는 상상할 수가 없었다. 


즉, RE:2와 RE:4가 존재하는 마당에 굳이 RE:3를 해야 할 이유는 없어보인다. 그럼에도 불구하고 등장하는 캐릭터들은 나름의 개성이 있고, 플레이 한 7시간이 아깝다고 느껴지지도 않아 바이오하자드 시리즈를 좋아한다면 추천할 수 있을 것 같다.

RE3 is a great remake overall. The graphics look fantastic, the gameplay feels smooth, and in general it’s a really fun experience from start to finish.
The main downside is that it feels pretty short when compared to other RE games. Also, whoever designed the final Nemesis fight on Inferno difficulty genuinely needs to be locked up... that fight had me rethink my life choices (pure suffering). 

Still, despite that, I would say I had a great time with the game.

This is just way worse then the original in almost every aspect except for the dodge mechanic and graphics. 


My main issue is just the fact that so many memorable locations have been removed and I also hate the fact that my gameplay gets interrupted by cutscenes all the time and I really don't feel like I have that "open world" that I can get lost in and solve puzzles like the original RE3.
Game was good. But the story is way too short for my liking unfortunately

The red headed step child of the modern Resident Evil games. While not bad, RE3 struggles from poor enemy design, a very on rails action movie presentation, and a poor hybrid of action/survival horror. It feels like the team from the beginning set out with an amateur plan, cutting out portions of the original, dumbing down and stream lining the challenging but satisfying gameplay of RE2, and hoping action set pieces make up for the lack of depth. Overall, I would say RE3 has some great features, up to the standard set by the franchise, but also misses the mark on so many other aspects. It will always sit as a "what could have been" entry for the series.

All that being said, this is a fun and polished game. Its more than worthy of its current price tag. It just doesn't hold up when compared to the other remakes.

Having played the OG Resident Evil 3 on the PSOne, I went into the remake with high expectations, and while it does change a lot from the classic version, I still really enjoyed it. The game looks fantastic, plays smoothly, and does a great job bringing Jill Valentine’s escape from Raccoon City into a modern cinematic experience.
I played it twice, The first play through I didn't know when you take down Nemesis it drops an item🤣🤣🤣

The action feels fast and intense, and Nemesis still brings that pressure and excitement whenever he appears. Jill was handled really well, and her stronger personality made the story even more engaging. I do wish it had included more content from the original and been a bit longer, but what’s here is polished and fun from start to finish. It may not fully capture everything that made the original special, but it’s still a very enjoyable Resident Evil game and a solid remake in its own way.

RE3 has become one of my more favorite RE games. I really enjoyed the faster-paced action horror side of resident evil. The visuals are very near photo realistic, and a crumbling racoon city is at its best in this game. Compared to RE2 where you're confined to the interiors of the iconic RCPD station. As Jill, you're outside, trying to stay alive in the different backdrops of the city, feeling like you're apart of the chaos of a falling Racoon city. Combat and movement feels a lot more fluid compared to RE2 remake, like the perfect polishing on an already great system. Dodging, No knife durability, an enhanced tyrant, as well as the levels' designs were all great additions that gave RE3 its on distant identity apart from RE2.

Jill Valentine is my favorite protagonist, Carlos is my favorite side character of the whole franchise. Jill is equally badass as she is endearing, and she's determined as hell. I loved following her lore as one of the few remaining STARS left investigating and dedicated to bringing down Umbrella. Carlos is the perfect foil to Jill, as a UBCS, Carlos finds himself in internal conflict as he's introduced to Jill, who is pessimistic about the motivations of Umbrella, and as they are forced into working together, they become an unstoppable dynamic duo who play very well off each other.

Nemesis is a big highlight, though my favorite pursier type is still Mr. X, My favorite boss battles easily go to Nemesis. His presence still adds pressure and instantly elevate the stakes. The boss battles are some of the coolest action set pieces and most outlandish spectacles I've ever had the pleasure to experience. Definitely memorable.

That said, the game's biggest weakness is its length. If RE2 was overbearing in it's length, then RE3 is just too rushed. From what I understand, there was a lot of cut content from the OG game that never made it to the remake. Despite never have played that game, I really do feel it as there are moments where there are more questions than answers, and levels that felt a bit rushed/streamlined.

My favorite of the RE: engine game so far, but would've been perfect if there was more time given to the story to be fleshed out
8/10

the game is hard asf for some reason but is also very short and i dont think it contributed to the resident evil series much also has bad optimization crashes on my high end pc no matter the settings 4.5/10

Personally I much more preferred this from the 2nd game, felt much more balanced in my flaws from the last game which was the lack of resources and the zombies did NOT feel as frustrating and tanky to fight. I really enjoyed this game and the graphics were phenomenal in this remaster. 9/10

Is it a good game? Yes. Is it worth paying anything over $15? No. The game is a very incomplete copy and paste of Resident Evil 2 Remake's code that can be completed in 4 hours on a blind playthrough. In fact, I was able to complete the game in its entity in 7 hours, and I'm sure it is able to be done much faster. You're better off buying the OG Resident Evil Nemesis or just playing Resident Evil 2 Remake.

The game is horror resemblant, but really has trouble keeping the suspense of Nemesis. RE2 perfected such tension with Mr. X but fails to keep that same formula with Nemesis. This game is a very noticeable deviation from the other Resident Evil Remakes that just makes the player feel like something is missing.

Speaking of something missing, the game cuts a lot of content from the original that is kind of confusing why it was cut. To save time? To save developer resources? Who knows. Many of the players of the OG RE3 loved the game in major part to the clock tower portions. In substitution for the clock tower section, there is a very sorry excuse for a boss fight. 

Just buy RE2

For what it is its fine, but its definitely lacking is some aspects. The biggest flaw is of course the length its around 3 hours, compared to the 12 of RE2R, its also not at all like the original RE3, it scrapped the roaming nemesis for scripted epic scenes, don't get me wrong its cool but i like the roaming nemi. in all id give it a 6,5/10
Its fun, you never stay in one place very long, lots of call backs to re2 story is weaker and the game is much shorter, but i must play if you liked re2
I think this game is very beautiful and the characters are lovable, but unfortunately the time to complete the game is very short and it's almost like DLC .
kinda short game & nemesis is kinda boring, but apart from that it's a good game, definitely overhated
To be honest I love resident evil and RE 2 remake showed me that but this game made me fall even more because of the characters and evil ahh bioweapon chasing me!
highly condensed game with great graphics Jill is peak only cons is that it's way too short compared to other RE games lol 8/10

As far as being a remake of Resident Evil 3, this totally butchers the original. Everyone knows this by now.

Aside from that, whilst the RE Engine looks fantastic and feels super polished, in basically every other aspect this game is just not fun to play. There is maybe one fun combat encounter in the entire game, the bosses suck, the puzzles suck, most of the fighting sucks and the enemies are not interesting enough to fight in general. The dodge is pretty good, and feels good to use when you get it right but even regular zombies can chain two attacks which would make the second unavoidable if you dodge away from the first. Most of the Nemesis encounters are more or less quicktime event cutscenes. Jill feels super bland, totally lacks the bite she had in the original; she's not really fulfilling any satisfying character archetype here. Not an unstoppable badass, not the plucky newbie. The other characters were written better but nothing really grabbed my attention.

Despite being a short game it really felt like it dragged and by the end I just wanted to slog through whatever was left so I could put the controller down. The way they chopped and swapped around the story ruined the pacing too

Ngl I actually had a really great time with this and it was a blast to play from start to finish. This remake is quite different to the RE2 Remake though, in that there are far less puzzles, far less backtracking, far more ammo, and overall it's just more of an action horror vs traditional survival horror. That's not a bad thing though, and I personally liked the change of pace.

Graphics, locations, cutscenes, atmosphere - all top notch. You really do feel immersed in the setting, and seeing Racoon City in modern graphics is always a treat.

Like everyone has said tho, it's REALLY short - so perhaps more worth it whilst on sale rather than at full price. There's also very little in terms of replay value so, unless you plan on hunting down every achievement/collectible, it's definitely a one-and-done kinda game.

It's alright as a game but not a good remake, just felt more like a DLC to RE2R considering the amount of cut contents from the original and reuse assets. Also Nemesis 4 in Inferno Mode doing the slam combo makes me want to EXPLODE in taliban style
good game, but so little bit hint/tutorial that very hard to learn by newbie player
Solid game, especially if you get it on sale. It could have benefited from having more in it. It's not my favorite RE but I do like it quite a bit, the combat is fun.

Definitely need to start with the disclaimer that I haven't played the OG and that this is my first RE game with Jill in it. With that said, I don't see why this game gets so much flack in the community. I realize the main complaint is that it's too different from the OG but if you want to play that one it's still there, it's even available on Steam now. This remake looks lovely, the gameplay is fun and feels like a perfect stepping stone between the remakes of 2 and 4, and it's hard not to love Jill and Carlos. I was even a bit attached to Nemesis by the end of it, but I took a lot of joy in defeating him. My only regret is that I didn't play this one before Requiem, because there are a few references that would've made more sense.

Imagine being chased by a giant bio-weapon while a nuclear missile is about to wipe out the entire city, and still having the courage to say, "Go, I'll handle this." Jill is an absolute legend.

My only real complaint is that the game is way too short. It ended just when I wanted more. Hopefully we'll get to see Jill again in future Resident Evil games.
I overall enjoyed this game but it did get a little repetitive after a while. Either way I recommend it though.
Almost got a heart attack on Assisted Difficulty, these games aren't for me. 10/10 would play again tho coz ♥♥♥♥ my heart.
Weakest RE remake so far but still pretty fun and worth it on deep sales. 8/10.

I didn't find much enjoyment out of this game and it hurts me to say this because I wanted to like this game. I came in with an open mind hoping that I can find enjoyment in this but I didn't and that's sad.

game is good. 
game mechanics are cool.
game enemies are bullet sponges.
game is short, too short, shorter than you somehow

very frustrating game especially if you are playing this right after RE2R. When the gameplay isn't annoying and frustrating, it is way too easy with save points every five minutes. It's really more of an action game than a horror game. Dodge mechanic is downright AWFUL, non-zombie enemies don't telegraph their attacks (frog things in the sewers especially) so you're literally just trial-and-error trying to figure out when the dodge timing is. Almost every large enemy can one shot you from full health if you miss a dodge. Scripted events where you're supposed to dodge or run in another direction are unclear and often make no sense whatsoever and you will die over and over before you figure out what you're supposed to do. And then after you die ten times to this BS you get a prompt to switch your difficulty to assisted that you can't change back after accidentally selecting yes when you're trying to retry a section.

All this in a game with 5 hours of content. Not to mention the reused police station map from RE2! Do not buy this crap for more than 5 dollars. I think I paid $10 and I still feel I wasted my money.

Absolute horsecrap of a story. Arc doesn't make tons of sense, and the Nemesis is annoying at best. Health is erratic, especially when you're being bitten and beaten by the Nemesis but then you get up like nothing had happened. Clunkiest evade mechanic ever. If you're not facing the enemy and hit the button at the exact time, you're done. No prompt to evade either, just need to learn when...or not. Always in "constraint mode" (what I mean by this is that you're always strapped up for inventory space, and choices DO matter when it comes to the ammo and its type. Nemesis has far too much health, and I just tried in normal mode. Also, there's no "fun". It's just the story, a couple puzzles thrown in but that's it, no vendor, no real feel of progression, no in-game challenges. Huge letdown, thank goat it was on sale. I'll try to finish the game, but not going to replay it like RE2 or RE 4.

Edit: finished the game...such a crap story. Visually beautiful, but the script has been written by a 13yo fanfictioning they were Jill or Carlos, with an autistic penchant for monosyllabic words, bar the occasional, really weird lexical sortie in a middle of a dialogue. Corny af, like the ending, and its music.
good game but inferior to the original due to cut content.

This is my first Resident Evil game, my only prior exposure to the franchise being the first movie featuring Milla Jovovich, which I liked but didn't like enough to watch all the movies.

But this game is simply incredible. Being a newcomer, I can't compare it to the other RE games, and for those who say this game is short, it certainly is, even I managed to beat it under 7 hours. But personally, being short is not a bad thing at all, in fact, I would hope all games were this short, though only if they know what they're doing like RE3.

So structure-wise, this game is like three movies, each complete with mini-acts, which makes the whole experience extremely enjoyable. One would think, before one jumps into the series that all the complex area exploration and survival horror would make the game, if not dull, moody and slow to say the least, but nothing could be further from the truth. The game is like a roller coaster ride, thanks to well-designed areas and sequences inserted at just the right moments that really spiced things up.

While the complexity of the areas certainly means this is hardly a linear game, it still managed to be extremely cinematic, thanks to the points I mentioned above. It is overall simply an incredibly tight, tense, fun and enjoyable experience.
I'm gonna recommend it because its not that its a bad game however it feels weaker compared to the other RE games , it feels like a demo . Loved Jill tho <3
It's a good game. I enjoyed it all, and Nemesis always chasing me was very fun. I'll just cooked him fr but Mr X is still scarier though. Worth it and I love it

great game. I know OG is better, but give this game a chance. Especially in Nightmare and Inferno mode. It feels super short, I wish Capcom haven't released it as an afterthought of re 2. Justice for Jill! Would love to see her again in their newer releases! Honestly loved this Jill so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ much!

It is definitely a good game, but compared to 2 remake it is noticeably rushed, with a lot of reused content from that game. It is also way shorter than either of the first 2 games, with RE1 taking me about 14 hours, RE2 taking me about 6, and this game taking me just over 3 hours to complete. Granted I didn't play it very thoroughly though. 

Overall it is a very good game though.

Decent game , but abit too easy. Too much ammo to play with, too short of a game, other RE games are better than this, even the original is better. 

Only buy on a discount , 5/10 game

Good game, I'm really enjoying it. I've heard it's a terrible remake, but since I never played the original, I can't comment on that. It's definitely not as good as the second one, though. The story and the atmosphere aren't nearly as good. Gameplay-wise, there is a strange change I don't understand, which is the removal of the defensive item slot. But the dodge is a nice addition and probably makes up for it. The graphics are really good. Overall, while it's not as good as the second game, I still think it's a good one.

I'm gonna compare this game to RE2 Remake a lot. All of us know that RE3 was riding the gravy train of RE2's success, so CAPCOM apparently didn't put as much effort into this game as they did with RE2.

I haven't played the original, but this game isn't bad at all, it's way too overhated by those geeky purists who keep comparing it to the original. Was the game "short"? Not really. I'd say it's almost the same length as RE2. You could definitely finish it in one sitting. Was this game beautiful enough to make you cream a bit? Most definitely, especially with RTX on. And don't get me started with the RE Engine. I've never seen such a stable and flat frame graph in my life with FPS reaching the sky. CAPCOM killed it with the graphics, optimization and the engine.

With that being said, this game is the polar opposite of RE2. This game is wayyy more action oriented, while RE2 was more horror/terror oriented. You could go nuking the whole city with an RPG with no fear of Nemesis sneaking up to you. It's a complete psychological overhaul where there's no consequences of walking too fast/loudly, shooting guns that creates noise etc. So coming from RE2, I had to completely change my playstyle.

At first I thought the dodge mechanic would be a saving grace as that didn't exist in RE2 but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ is it hard to perfect it. 9/10 times you'll just dodge too early or too late, heck, maybe dodge TOWARDS the enemy who's trying to kill you.

Here's a nitpick about RE games in general: the pointless habit of doing headshots. Logically you'd think one headshot would be fatal and kill those ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ instantly, but nahhhhh, sometimes you'd be shooting 10 bullets and they still won't die, so ♥♥♥♥ THAT.

My boi Carlos is the comic relief of the entire game. He's just a likeable dude yk? He'd make those cheesy yet funny Leon oneliners, make random remarks about random things, and playing as him with his assault rifle really sealed the deal for me.

Last but not least, Nemesis. He's a scripted enemy trying to be a stalker enemy like Mr. X, that's it. This ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ is more annoying that scary to me. He could punch you like 5 times at once without giving you the chance to move, he'll pull you with this tentacle ♥♥♥♥ (i think?), he'll jump/dash across the map and teleport right infront of you, and did I mention this ♥♥♥♥ owning an RPG? Yeah, doesn't sound like Mr. X at all, does it? X was a gentleman, while Nemesis is a scripted barbarian. The good thing is that after you leave the main raccoon city area, he mostly disappears and stops being a threat but comes up in differently stages/forms later in the game. The first stage was the worst and the most annoying though.

Good game.
its okay. I understand its a let down. It's definitely no where near resident evil 2 remake for me.
It's a very good game overall. My only complaint is that it feels a bit short, although it has great replay value.

I'd recommend it if you're a Resident Evil fan. This feels more like RE2 DLC rather than a standalone title. The biggest issue for me is that the game is too linear for me. It feels like it took more inspiration from Cash Bandicoot rather than being a very open area with multiple pathways. They do exist, of course. But compared to the prior games and after, it's obvious.

Ad for the length, it's very short. Isn't an issue since most RE games can be beaten is less than 4 hours or even 3 if you're good enough. But for a standard playthrough, I beat this game in 4 hours (replayed it in my 2nd playthrough) and I was kinda disappointed that the game ended just like that. 

But I like this game regardless. I just consider it as an expensive DLC for RE2 or add-on to it considering how short it is for standard/new playthroughs.

I don't get the hate, sure its smaller but its so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ badass.
Jill is so badass I love her.
9/10

My first game that I actually fully completed from the resident evil franchise, and it honestly was a solid tense, yet fun short ride. I can't remember why I chose to start with the franchise from this entry. I don't really know much of the overall lore, and entering the series in the middle of the arc was probably not the smartest choice, but the little snippets of media and exposure to the community helped to at least get an idea of whats going on and not get lost; the story was ok at best, carried heavily by the charm of its two protagonists jill and carlos. Gameplay wise, gun combat is decent, the game tries to push you to save your resources to create a sense of tension but I never really found that effective until the very end of the game. The game gets you with a jumpscare or two but gets predictable quickly. Mind you I never played the OG resident evil 3 so all the criticism about how the game fails to represent the Original game doesn't really affect my experience. The game was challenging enough to keep you engaged, and roughens up a little bit near towards the end. While the boss fights were nothing crazy, they were engaging. The game runs smoothly and no bugs were faced. Overall would recommend under a big discount.

It is a real shame they cut so much content from the original game. The length is definitely disappointing, to the point where a solid speedrun can take less than an hour to finish. However, I still recommend picking it up on a sale because grinding out all the achievements was pretty fun and I enjoyed the constant action.
the grastest game ive evre played i just started and i just beat it best game i recomend this game

Only buy this game when it's on sale. The amount of content in the game is not worth 40 dollars. In comparison, RE2 remake is also 40 dollars yet it took me 13 extra hours to beat it. Besides the price and shortness of the game, it is good. A lot of OG Resident Evil fans are upset that the chase scenes are limited because in the original Nemesis would show up at random. However, I don't like being chased therefore I like this mechanic. I wish the game was fully remade for RE3 like how RE2 was. It's a fun game, but only worth it when it costs less.

Definitely preferred RE2. RE3 seemed to emphasize combat more with the dodge mechanic which I liked for boss fights but not so much for standard zombies. I feel they really ramped up the difficulty to compensate for this mechanic. There's also a bunch of stronger, but not boss enemies in this game that are frankly annoying as hell with one-shot potential. Didn't expect to get hit by a 3-hit combo in a Resident Evil game. 

Anyways, I think this focus on combat isn't horrible, but it's not for me (and mind you, I love action games). The tension from running out of resources and strategically thinking about how to proceed was fun in RE2. I went into this game with a strategy I used in RE2: try to rush past zombies rather than kill them to save ammo. Went terribly, as you can see from my playtime. The game gives you a ton of ammo compared to RE2, and I did so much better after I realized this. I think it would've been interesting if the Carlos sections were more combat-focused while Jill sections played more like RE2. 

The other aspects of the game were alright, pretty standard RE stuff, and I'm sure many already heard the issues around the game saying it's a bad remake and all that. But overall, not a bad deal if you can buy it for 5-10 dollars.

Shorter than the other RE remakes, but about the same length as the classic RE3. They've refined the basic gameplay formula with this one. It plays excellently.

Some other reviews complain that they cut locations & puzzles from the classic game. IMO, for each location and puzzle they cut, they replaced it with something equally as good if not better. So it doesn't really feel like they cut things for budget, but, rather, that they refined and tweaked the flow of the overall game to better match the narrative they were trying to tell. Expanding in some areas (more detailed hospital to sell the idea of a "ground zero"), and cutting areas that didn't feed that narrative in the original game (the park).

That said, I do love that they still kept references to all those locations in-game, and still followed the basic skeleton of the original story. There are several locations in this new game that have almost exactly the same layout as in the original (usually areas that you pass through quickly, so you'd miss it if you didn't know what to look for).

One complaint: they don't do as good a job with Nemesis as in the original. While the big set-pieces are great, the aspect of Nemesis I loved about the original is that he could appear almost at random while you were out exploring, forcing you to think about the layout of the map and how to get to your destination while avoiding him. Also, the loot he drops for optionally taking him down isn't as interesting as in the original game. Which is a shame because the game does have some interesting items it could drop, but they're actually part of the unlockable point system, rather than part of the core game. I'd have loved if knocking out Nemesis a third time gave you the Samurai Edge pistol (a recreation of the M92F from the classic game), or Jill's RE1 costume, rather than having to unlock those via the points system.

This game is basically a watered down RE2.  It has all the same systems and mechanics as RE2 but the implementation is so much worse.  The downtown section is not as good as the RPD from RE2.  NEST 2 was far worse the NEST from RE2.  The sewers are worse than the ones from RE2.  And you guessed it the underground section was not as good as the one from RE2.  To top if off there is a massive amount of cut content from the original RE3 including the diverging path mechanic and the phase 2 Nemesis boss fights are just so annoying.
this game genuinely sucks ass the only redeeming qualities are the characters

Incredible remake of a great game, however a lot of content from RE3 was cut. This game is very high quality, arguably better than Resident Evil 2. This game builds on top of the previous game and allows you to revisit some locations. The game itself is spectacular and is well worth the full price.

Resident evil 3 (Biohazard) remake is inkranneble game and it has inkranable story.

In case of a remake, it´s obvious in rating, but as a standalone game I´d say it´s very decent.
Beside the obvious glaring issue, the next problem in line is the lenght of the game, wouldn´t buy at a full price. The game is too short for any of the locations to stick, beside the first half of the game in Racoon city map. None of the locations feel fleshed out and are too short to leave an opinion for you, except the hospital, that part couldn´t take longer. The shortness of the game really did a number of issues with the pacing, enemy variety and story. 
Why would you want to play as both Jill and Carlos if their playstyles are so similar, what´s the point then. Speaking of the beautiful king, his parts really didn´t feel necesarry, the only main part of his playthrough is in the hospital, which you will still get to go through as Jill. Good thing he´s handsome.
The dodge mechanic speaks for itself I think. There really isn´t a clear enough indicator to get the perfect dodge, and there ALSO isn´t an indicator for the one shot move the lizard frogs are doing, epic. The dodge could be forgiven, if Jill wasn´t so agonizingly slow.
Nemesis is the most impossing only at the beggining, the big dog form or the big pile of thrash didn´t do it for me. But the first part of the game in Racoon city is really the best part, scary, tense and fun, Nemesis spawncamping is still funny.
The game is good, enjoyed myself, but it really is just too short.

I really liked this game but the length is atrocious for a $40 price tag. There was so much potential to be great but it feels a bit rushed and like it just wanted to get itself over with ASAP. 

Nemesis is a scary looking dude but Mr. X is still the king in terms of anxiety inducing fear factor and sheer presence.
good graphics, but the story is way too short. Buy when it's on sale it is not worth buying for the full price.
I didn't play the original. A short but successful game. Unlike its other games, there is more action in this game. Its graphics and engine are beautiful.

The best way to describe this game is short and sweet. While it's cut out a lot of content from the original game, I still had a really enjoyable time with it! Carlos is much more charming here than in the original game, and keeps a light hearted mood. Nemesis is really intimidating and difficult to escape! Like an octopus Mr X on steroids. All in all its a good time and fun to replay while attempting to complete challenges and gain steam awards!

*Only get this game when it is on sale.*

It is not worth it at full price. I would say that there is enough replay value due to the other difficulties you can play. I'm currently trying to go for an S Rank run on all difficulties... and yes, including Inferno.

In addition, it is quite an efficient game to 100% because the game is incredibly short. Other than that, this game is mostly just fine as a Resident Evil game. The game still offers challenging gameplay in some areas, mostly through resource management. Figuring out how much ammo you need to save, which enemies to avoid, and how to stock up efficiently so you do not end up soft-locking yourself at a boss fight can be quite engaging.

The dialogue in this game is bad to an extent, but I have come to accept that Resident Evil has always had its fair share of cliché and cheesy dialogue. Funny enough, one of my favourite games of all time is Resident Evil 4 Remake, so I can't say much lol.

The game's narrative is okay. It is mostly just chase scene after chase scene after chase scene. However, I believe the original also focused on that element, but with better intention. It made Nemesis an actual terrifying villain rather than just some annoying foe you have to deal with to progress through the game.

The game still explores themes of corporate greed, resilience, and survival against inevitability. It is about dealing with problems that seem impossible—not necessarily defeating them, but overcoming them and learning how to face the world's larger issues. The game also masterfully (kind of) transitions from urban and community-centred areas to a large industrial complex. It creates a contrast between the human world and the artificial world.

Homes are replaced by laboratories. Communities are replaced by corporations working together not just for survival, but for financial gain through human exploitation. Nature has been turned into a testing ground for Umbrella, and humanity itself can now be viewed as a bioweapon due to the large-scale weaponisation seen throughout the Resident Evil series. An example of this is Nemesis, who was created as a weapon to hunt down the remaining S.T.A.R.S. members.

Yet overall, the game is just fine. I have not played the original, but I can say that this remake is simply okay on the surface. Sure, I had fun, and I do not mind replaying it to earn all the achievements and beat every difficulty. However, if you are looking for a true Resident Evil remake experience, play Resident Evil 2 or Resident Evil 4. Those remakes are far better than this one.

Final Score: 6/10

not a bad game and while it is pretty fun it could be a lil boring in my opinion its also well optimised which is a plus.
overall id say it was a 6.4/10

If you have never played the original game I would highly recommend this remake.  I have only played this resident evil game so I can't compare it to any others but it's a great introduction for people just trying to get into the series. I would recommend getting this game on sale to try it. If your computer is not as powerful as recommended you will need to turn down every aspect of the graphics. I have had room deload before I turned down the settings.
A little shorter than I thought it would be, but a lot of fun! Also A LOT scarier than RE2 at some points iykyk

Resident Evil 3 is a thrilling action-horror experience that successfully reimagines a classic game while delivering fast-paced gameplay and memorable set pieces. Playing as Jill Valentine as she struggles to escape the doomed Raccoon City is both exciting and tense, especially thanks to the constant threat of Nemesis, one of the series' most iconic monsters.

The game's visuals are excellent, with detailed environments, impressive character models, and atmospheric lighting that bring the city to life. Jill is a strong and likable protagonist, and her interactions with Carlos Oliveira add personality and heart to the story. The voice acting and cinematic presentation help make the narrative engaging from beginning to end.

Combat feels smooth and responsive, with the dodge mechanic adding a satisfying layer of skill that rewards good timing. Resource management remains important, but the game leans more heavily into action than some other entries in the series. The hospital section, in particular, stands out as one of the strongest and most intense parts of the game.

While some fans may be disappointed that certain locations and features from the original game were cut or shortened, the remake still offers an enjoyable and polished experience. Its shorter length encourages multiple playthroughs, and the higher difficulty modes provide a rewarding challenge for players looking to master the game.

Overall, Resident Evil 3 is a fun and exciting entry in the franchise. It may not reach the same heights as Resident Evil 2 Remake, but it delivers strong characters, excellent gameplay, and plenty of memorable moments. For both newcomers and longtime fans, it is well worth playing.
I have to say, after completing the game and earning all the trophies, I found it to be a somewhat underwhelming experience compared to RE2. Nonetheless, it was still an enjoyable game overall.
The pace of the game is super slow and story is not very interesting.

I'm playing the series for the first time, 1 remaster, 2 remake, now 3 remake. This is the first not great game I've played in the series. It's not terrible, but a big step down compared to 2 remake. It's lacking the atmosphere and deliberate pacing of 2. I never used the knife or dodging, not necessary. I killed all the zombies except some hoards in the first section. There are no parts where you're scared of a new enemy.

It also has some shocking lack of care/attention. Jill has an invisible flashlight. When you go to a dark area, a beam of light just appears in front of her, but she's not holding a flashlight. 2 remake does not have this problem. When you get the laser sight upgrade for a weapon, it just adds a red dot TEXTURE in the center of the screen, instead of an actual laser beam. 2 remake has an actual laser beam.

If you're going through the series do pick this up for the biggest discount possible and play it, but don't expect quality.

The game cuts some content from the original; but made up for it with modernisation and some improvements in the story compared to the original. Overall, if you're a Resident Evil fan you would enjoy this game as it is a strong addition to the series.

прошел сразу после второй части и с первых минут удивился. поначалу вообще подумал, что играю в какой-то мод на subway surfers с немезисом. безумная динамика и экшен — последнее, чего я ожидал от resident evil.

играется легко и бодро, а знакомые локации из 2 remake приятно греют сердце. хорошая линейная игра без лишней духоты. рекомендую как отличный десерт после прохождения второй части.

Is this as good as RE2 and RE4 remake? Of course not and the game is completely different. That being said this is not a bad game I very much enjoyed my time. If it was made by the same studio that did RE2 and RE4 then my god would they have one crazy trilogy of remakes. But still its good.

Overall rating: 9 out of 10.

This game's pacing is actually fun and you don't get burnt out unlike RE2. Idk why people hate on it, both characters are likeable and actually have chemistry. I like it a lot more than RE2. Carlos come back
Seems like the community likes to hate this one but I had fun even with it being short and missing content. Loved the dodge mechanics

After earning the Platinum Trophy and completing every possible challenge, the one thing that truly stands out is how well Nemesis was designed. With modern-day graphics, Nemesis looks absolutely phenomenal.
The story is, of course, the same as in the original Resident Evil 3, but the remake removed many of the memorable and sentimental moments that made the original so special. Even the scenes and elements that were kept and reworked barely resemble their counterparts from the original game. Some locations are so heavily redesigned that you wouldn't even recognize them unless you stopped and took a careful look around.
Although this is considered one of the weaker remakes in the Resident Evil series, that doesn't mean it's a bad game. It simply could have been handled much better in a way that would have reminded longtime fans of the original of their childhood memories and all the great moments they experienced while playing it.
Capcom really shows no mercy when it comes to the Platinum Trophy, as the achievements in this game are extremely demanding. You have to replay the game many times to unlock everything.
A new difficulty level is unlocked with each completed playthrough, and you need to finish the game on all difficulty levels to earn the Platinum Trophy.
Fortunately, the game can be completed in about two hours—or even less—once you've played through it a few times.

Score: 6.5/10
This game is very good in my opinion definetly reccomend it to someone who likes survival horror or new players, can also run on low end computers with good settings

i know many people hated to this game because this game has a very short story compared to the og, but for a player who never played the original like me its still very fun game. But still im recommended to buy this games on discount

Played this game over and over again for the fun gunplay and the one liners. I found myself booting up this game and replaying the story because it was fun and it only takes a couple hours. 

It's not a perfect game but its really fun for what it is - if the game is on sale, get it. My biggest complaint is that there are no minigames, which is a shame because the guns are really fun in this game.

This game really dig in on action and cinematic whereas the original has a lot of content just sad to say if they add the other sequences from the original this ♥♥♥♥ would be good but remake is a remake but kinda sad they forgot to add the clock tower..

i bought it as a bundle with re2 and i was shocked how short it was. i thought ppl are messing around lol. i finished this game in two days which never in my life do so. even so, its a good fast pace game. one thing i wanna complain is the fact carlos doesnt exists anywhere after this game. im so sad cause hes cute and cool but yall dont wanna take advantage of that. if i could give a star out of 5, i would choose 4 cause again, carlos doesnt exists apart of this game.

Honestly, I have been struggling to understand where to even put RE3 in terms of any sort of review. On the one hand, as someone who has not played the original it was serviceable in the aspect of filling in a couple blanks for the continuation of the story, but it also is mostly prevalent that this game was definitely not supposed to be a RE3 ACTUAL remake but rather some sort of additional content for 2, flavoured with RE3. This game even as someone who has not played the original is not my favourite for many reasons and most of the things I do like are just borrowed from the RE2 remake so it doesn’t stand out. BESIDES JILL, i am not kidding this game gets brownie points purely for the fact that jill is in it and she looks really good and sounds really good. Gameplay wise this game is like overly linear for a Resident Evil game, the “paint” is a straight line, there might have been like…1 or 2 puzzles? I don’t even remember if they were they weren’t even really puzzles rather just backtracking. The actual like “new” stuff such as the dodging system are just not fun at all, it feels extremely jank and awkward at times. Hitboxes felt like sometimes I absolutely should or should not have been able to dodge something and would have rather just been able to basically dodge myself by moving left and right instead of having a dedicated dodge system if they were going to implement it like that. The pacing of this game to what I thought would happen was incredibly bad, this game is too fast for the story, so many just random cuts in a way or shortcuts that are bringing me through the game at a fast pace, not to mention the weird handful of continuity errors.
I think I spent more time doing the chess puzzle in RE2 the first time then I did playing raccoon city and sewers entirely in 3make. Nemesis also is a joke of a stalker in this game, just entirely scripted and you can lose him way too easily when he does show up, if he was like Mr.X in RE2 that would’ve been a lot cooler but the game is too short to have him show up like that anyway, maybe they could have but also maybe not. I am aware of the cut areas in the game, but even besides that the areas that are in the game themselves really are shorter, they are so unbelievably short in fact that I think I beat the game first try in just barely over 3 hours which is not good for a Resident Evil game. The game is not very buggy at the very least, and you get to learn about some parts of RE3 which is also a plus, it runs well and I got the game for $5 so I PERSONALLY can’t complain too much about that, I just have gripes with it after playing the first two RE games. Like I said though, serviceable, but seriously after coming right off RE2 remake this game is not the best, but if I had to be entirely unbiased and just looked at it as one product it actually gets more points knowing its a Resident Evil game, even though the actual gameplay is a barebones version of the first two, it is OK and Jill gets it an extra point, 6/10 and thats being absolutely generous.
Another great RE Remake! making the games more accessible with far superior controls and less difficultly thank god!
